channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon detained in massive law-enforcement effort
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was detained in a massive law enforcement operation at the presidential compound Wednesday morning, saying he complied with the warrant after weeks of defiance over attempts to question him in the investigation over his imposi
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: SEC sues Elon Musk, saying he didn't disclose Twitter ownership on time before buying it
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has sued billionaire Elon Musk, saying he failed to disclose his ownership of Twitter stock in a timely manner in early 2022, before buying the social media site.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at the terms
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — If the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal goes according to the current draft, then fighting will stop in Gaza for 42 days, and dozens of Israeli hostages and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners will be freed.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lucy Walker made a searing film about wildfires in 2021. Now, people may be more inclined to listen
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When Lucy Walker debuted her harrowing documentary about California wildfires, “Bring Your Own Brigade,” at Sundance in 2021, it was during peak COVID. Not the best time for a film on a wholly different scourge. “It was really hard,” the Oscar-nominated filmmaker says now.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justin Baldoni tells Disney, Marvel: Save ‘Deadpool & Wolverine’ docs
item.description: Baldoni's lawyer said there's "no question" Ryan Reynolds was "making fun" of Baldoni with his "Nicepool" character.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. attorneys for Michigan resign before Trump takes office
item.description: Both of Michigan's U.S. attorneys have announced they will resign from their posts before Donald Trump is sworn into office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Britain's Princess of Wales says her cancer is in remission
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales said Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after she spent the day supporting other cancer patients at the hospital where she received treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Firefighters deploy, more power is shut off ahead of fierce Southern California winds
item.description: A week after the first fires began, the flames have destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: NYC looks to cut emissions from charboilers at restaurants
item.description: Under the proposed rule, restaurants with charbroil grills would need to install an emissions control device.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Dementia cases expected to double by 2060: Study
item.description: Dementia cases in the U.S. are expected to double by 2060 when around 1 million Americans are projected to develop the memory-losing condition every year, according to a new study published Monday in the medical journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains found in backyard grave, man arrested for cold case murder
item.description: Murphy Henry, 54, was also charged with abuse of a corpse and tampering with evidence, related to the disappearance of Staggs.
item.dc:creator: Matt Rawlings, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles couple's harrowing escape as Eaton Fire approached their home caught on video doorbell
item.description: A California couple's Ring video doorbell captured their harrowing escape as the Eaton Fire approached their Altadena home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 29 dogs seized in Memphis animal cruelty case
item.description: Police said six dogs were located inside the residence on Staten, and the rest, including eight puppies, were in the yard.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: This popular hiking trail may hold clues to how Palisades Fire ignited
item.description: A beloved hiking spot north of Sunset Boulevard in Pacific Palisades is reportedly a point of interest for investigators looking into the origins of the costliest wildfire in the history of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Rashida Jones is stepping down as MSNBC president on the eve of Trump inauguration
item.description: MSNBC President Rashida Jones says she is stepping down after four years leading the liberal news network, her move coming on the eve of a second Trump administration and after changes in corporate ownership.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michelle Obama will not attend Trump's inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Veteran American Airlines flight attendant killed in random attack while on a layover in Denver
item.description: Police confirmed that a suspect is in custody after taking the life of an American Airlines flight attendant who was on a layover in Denver; the incident was one in a series of attacks over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he's leaving Trump with a 'strong hand to play' in world conflicts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that his stewardship of American foreign policy has left the U.S. safer and economically more secure, arguing that President-elect Donald Trump will inherit a nation viewed as stronger and more reliable than it was four years ago.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to save TikTok videos before the potential ban goes into effect
item.description: The deadline for a potential TikTok ban in the U.S. is fast approaching and many of the app's users are already taking steps to prepare, even as its fate remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Biden announces student debt relief for 150,000 borrowers, bringing total helped to more than 5M
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it was forgiving student loans for more than 150,000 borrowers, bringing the total number of individuals impacted by debt relief in President Biden’s term to over 5 million.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Taquitos sold at Aldi recalled after customer complains of 'dental injury': USDA
item.description: The recall specifically concerns 20-ounce packages of Casa Mamita Chicken & Cheese Taquitos produced by Bestway Sandwiches, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: Southern areas hit by winter storm thaw and power slowly returns
item.description: After a freezing winter storm shut schools, cut power and cancelled or delayed flights, the South was slowly thawing Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 16 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kohl's to close 27 stores by April as struggling department stores works to improve sales
item.description: Kohl’s said Friday it was closing 27 underperforming locations in 15 states by April — a fraction of its 1,150 store base — as the struggling department store chain aims to boost profitability and improve sagging sales.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gun jams as woman tries to shoot boyfriend: Tennessee police
item.description: A Tennessee woman is facing an attempted murder charge after reportedly attempting to shoot her boyfriend before her gun jammed, Memphis Police said.
item.dc:creator: Raven Moore, ABC 4
